summary: Full backend + frontend E2E verification of the new 'Dashboard Operativa' on /app/orders (3 ops cards + critical-first sort toggle + reliability dot). Backend: 12/12 pytest tests pass on the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL as demo@ordia.app. Frontend: full Playwright regression on /app/orders confirms the 3 ops cards render correct counts (green=3, amber=3, red=0), toggle bucket filter on/off, colored reliability dots match card colors on every filtered row, critical-first toggle reorders red→amber→green→done with reliability ASC within amber (68→72→86 verified), and all pre-existing filters (status filter-all/needs_review/ready/validated/exported, delivery-filter-*, orders-search, pagination, row detail navigation) still work and combine correctly with the new bucket + sort params.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1198 list_orders loads up to 1000 orders per request into memory to compute the summary + bucket + critical sort in Python. This scales fine for the current demo tenant (9 orders) and works for tenants under a few hundred orders, but once a company crosses ~1k orders per read, (a) the summary counts will silently be capped at 1000 (undercounting), and (b) memory + sort cost grows linearly. Not blocking for the current MVP, but before onboarding heavy tenants: move the summary counts to a Mongo aggregation ($group over a computed bucket) and add an index on (company_id, created_at desc). Also consider streaming the bucket assignment or adding a persisted 'bucket'/'reliability' field on the order document that is recomputed on write, which would let the sort + filter run entirely in Mongo.

server.py:1213 sort='critical' uses a Python .sort() on the already-loaded list; combined with the 1000-order cap this means the 'critical-first' order is only truly critical-first within the first 1000 orders. Once a tenant grows past that, silently truncated data would give wrong sort. Add an assertion/log when len(docs)==1000 so we notice the cap.

Orders.js:139 onCard resets filter to 'all' when a card is clicked, which is correct per spec, but the code does NOT reset the delivery filter or the search query — this is intentional (the request says card counts are influenced by q and delivery filter), but worth confirming with the product that clicking 'Da confermare' while a search is active only counts orders matching the search. Currently the backend summary IS computed on the same q+delivery-filtered set, so the count is consistent with what the user sees — GOOD, no bug, just calling it out.

Orders.js:120-122 the search debounce useEffect names its timer variable 't' which shadows the i18n translator 't' from useI18n() on line 116. Locally OK because the useEffect callback has its own scope, but it hurts readability. Rename to 'timer' or 'iv'.

Orders.js:130 the api call is fired every time page/filter/bucket/etc. change. The 'summary' object is returned on every response, meaning it will re-render the 3 cards on every keystroke of the search (debounced) and every pagination click. That's fine, and it's also what makes counts consistent with q+delivery filters — but it means the card count 'flickers' briefly during loading. Consider passing keepPreviousData or a small skeleton on the cards while loading — NON-BLOCKING.

Orders.js:46-51 ReliabilityDot uses a title tooltip for the % on non-xl screens (the % is only inline at xl breakpoint). Screen readers will announce the label via the title, which is acceptable, but consider adding aria-label for better a11y.

server.py:1160 the green bucket rule `status == validated OR (review == 0 AND pct >= 90)` — this means an order in status='ready' (not yet confirmed) with e.g. 100% confidence and 0 review lines will be green (Pronti da inviare). That is the intended behavior per spec, but note that a 'ready' order in this state has NOT been human-confirmed — the UI card copy 'Affidabili, li puoi inviare al gestionale' is slightly optimistic. If the product wants strict 'ready to ship = validated only', tighten the rule to `status == validated`. Non-blocking, matches current spec.

server.py:1156 reliability pct = round(mean(line_items.confidence) * 100) — a line item without a 'confidence' key contributes 0 to the mean, silently pulling the % down. If historical orders (imported/manual) don't set confidence, they will fall into red incorrectly. Consider defaulting missing confidence to None and excluding those lines from the mean (or defaulting to 1.0 for manually-added lines). Only matters if there are manual line-items with no confidence field.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 pytest passed: summary shape, bucket filter green/amber/red, sort=critical bucket + reliability ASC within bucket, sort=recent default, summary vs bucket-filter count coherence, bucket logic spec, combined filters, pagination)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 7 acceptance criteria + regression pass: 3 ops cards render + toggle bucket, dot color matches bucket per row, card count == filtered rows, critical-first reorder verified with actual bucket sequence [amber×3 → green×3 → done×3] and amber % ASC [68,72,86], status/delivery/search filters + row-detail navigation still work)

seed_data_creation: None. Used the 9 existing demo orders (3 exported/done + 3 validated-or-green + 3 needs_review/amber, no red). Verified the empty-state for red=0 is correctly rendered ('Nessun ordine trovato').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ops dashboard is fully working. To test 'red' bucket (not exercised in demo data because all amber orders are >=60%), create/ingest an order whose line_items average confidence is <0.6 — e.g. POST /api/orders/ingest with garbled text that Claude flags with low confidence, or PUT /orders/{id} with line_items containing 'confidence': 0.3. Then GET /api/orders?bucket=red should return that order and ops-card-red should show 1 with a red dot. The bucket-logic pytest is data-driven so it will keep validating as new orders are added. Also note: the backend caps the summary computation at 1000 orders per request — this is fine now but flagged as a scale concern in critical_code_review_comments. Fast bucket color detection in Playwright: red=bg-red-500, amber=bg-amber-500, green=bg-emerald-500 (not /60), done=bg-emerald-500/60, pending=bg-slate-300.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Feature works end-to-end. All 12 pytest cases pass and all Playwright UI assertions (card counts, dot colors, filter toggles, sort ordering, regression) succeed.
